questions.text: Which of the following is the correct sequence of events in DNA replication?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: DNA polymerase binds → Helicase unwinds DNA → Ligase joins Okazaki fragments → Primase adds RNA bases
Ligase joins Okazaki fragments → DNA polymerase binds → Helicase unwinds DNA → Primase adds RNA bases
Primase adds RNA bases → DNA polymerase binds → Helicase unwinds DNA → Ligase joins Okazaki fragments
Helicase unwinds DNA → Primase adds RNA bases → DNA polymerase binds → Ligase joins Okazaki fragments
Primase adds RNA bases → Helicase unwinds DNA → DNA polymerase binds → Ligase joins Okazaki fragments

questions.explanation: Firstly, the DNA is unwound by DNA helicase. This is followed by primers (RNA Bases), attaching themselves onto the DNA strand, to initiate replication. This step is crucial as DNA Polymerase can only attach new DNA nucleotides to an existing strand of nucleotides, provided by the primers. As soon as DNA polymerase binds to the primers, DNA replication is initiated. Lastly, the Okazaki fragments, of the lagging strand, are attached to the template DNA using DNA ligase.Hence option D would be the correct choice. The diagram, provided, may aid in understanding the process.

questions.text: Out of the following, which is the stage of meiosis during which sister chromatids are separated?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: During meiosis, the chromosomes remain replicated (i.e.,remain as two joined sister chromatids) for the entire first set of divisions. The whole point to the second set of meiotic divisions is to separate the sister chromatids. This takes place during anaphase II. You can remember that during anaphase things get pulled apart since they both begin with “A.”

questions.text: Homologous structures, which have similar underlying structures but may have different functions, are formed by:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Divergent evolution occurs when the same ancestral organism is placed into different environments and must then adapt to function in these different environments. Thus, the same original structures evolve separately and “diverge” from one another. Examples of these homologous structures are the arm of a man, the wing of a bat, and the flipper of a whale. All have the same basic bone structure but vastly different functions. The opposite of divergent evolution is convergent evolution, in which vastly different organisms are placed into the same environment and must adapt to perform similar functions with different structures. Convergent evolution produces analogous structures, examples of which are the wings of bats, the wings of birds, and the wings of butterflies. Speciation is often the result of divergent evolution, not the cause of it.

questions.text: Consider the graph of substrate concentration versus product formation shown below. Assume enzyme concentration to be constant. Why does the graph level off at high substrate concentrations?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: All the enzyme is used up, and product formation cannot occur without it
There is no more substrate to be converted into product
Substrate concentration exceeds enzyme concentration, and all active sites are saturated
The reaction has run to completion
An inhibitor has been added, and it has slowed down the rate of product formation

questions.explanation: When the concentration of substrate far exceeds the concentration of enzyme (remember, the question states that enzyme concentration is assumed to be constant), all of the enzyme active sites are saturated with substrate, and the product is being formed at its maximum rate. The only way to increase product formation at this point is to increase the concentration of the enzyme. Note that enzymes should not be used up in the course of the reaction, so (A) is wrong. Furthermore, product formation is still occurring, just at a stable rate, so (B) and (D) are wrong. There is no reason to assume an inhibitor has been added; the rate of product formation remains constant.

questions.text: Plants that have true roots, stems, and leaves, as well as flowers and seeds enclosed in fruit, are classified as:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Flowering plants are angiosperms. Bryophytes are mosses, so (A) is incorrect. Tracheophytes are non-seed-producing plants (ferns), so (B) is also wrong. Gymnosperms are conifers (naked seed plants), so (C) is incorrect. “Endosperm” is not a classification for plants.

questions.text: A population of birds (Population A) on a remote, isolated island is studied to determine beak length. The resulting data are plotted in Figure 1. Suppose that 200 years later, the beaks of the birds on the island were again measured (Population B). The data, when plotted, yielded a graph as in Figure 2. What is the most likely reason for the difference in distribution of beak lengths between the data plotted in Figure 1 and the data plotted in Figure 2?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: All birds with beaks of 30 mm flew to a new island over the 200-year time span
Birds with beaks of 30 mm were selected against by nature
Predators consumed birds with beaks of 40 mm
Predators consumed birds with beaks of 20 mm
Birds with beaks of 30 mm were selected for extinction

questions.explanation: Clearly, the birds with 30 mm beaks were not surviving too well. There is no reason to assume they flew to another island; remember, they are on a remote, isolated island. There may not be another island near enough to fly to, so (A) is incorrect. If predators consumed birds with 20 mm or 40 mm beaks, they would not be the prevalent populations in Figure 2, so (C) and (D) are wrong, and it is not possible for something to be selected for extinction. Things can only be selected to survive. If they are not selected then they don’t survive by default, which means that (E) can be eliminated.

questions.text: Choose the correct order of events in a persons response to a bacterial infection:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Macrophage ingestion → Helper T cell activation → B cell activation → clonal formation
Clonal formation → Helper T cell activation → B cell activation → macrophage ingestion
Macrophage ingestion → B cell activation → T helper cell activation → clonal formation
Helper T cell activation → B cell activation → macrophage ingestion → clonal formation
B cell activation → T helper cell activation → clonal formation → macrophage ingestion

questions.explanation: First, the bacteria is engulfed and ingested by a macrophage, via phagocytosis. The macrophage proceeds to present the residual antigen of the bacteria on it's surface.Then, Helper T cells become activated by interacting with antigen-presenting cells, the macrophages. Activation of the Helper T cells is triggered by the bacterial antigen, on macrophages' surfaces.This is followed by Helper T cells, inducing the activation of the B cell, through the binding of CD40L (Ligand), on the T cell, to CD40, on the B cell.Finally, due to their activation, B cells colonialize and produce antibodies against the pathogen.

questions.text: The following graphs show the growth of two closely related species of paramecia, both when grown alone (Figure 1) and when grown together (Figure 2). Both species consume bacteria as their food source and reproduce by binary fission as often as several times a day. The data in Figure 2 indicate that:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: P. aurelia is preying on P. caudata
P. aurelia is a better competitor than P. caudata
P. aurelia and P. caudata are in a symbiotic relationship
P. aurelia is a parasite of P. caudata
P. aurelia grew better when combined with P. caudata than it did when grown alone

questions.explanation: Same species have common requirements for natural resources and act as a competitor of each other. The overlapping niches induce competition and the species, best fitted to survive, comes on top. Figure 2 clearly shows that P. aurelia is a better competitor than P. caudata. If this were to be a symbiotic association, both species would have benefited but here, only P. aurelia is showing a better growth rate while P. caudata growth rate is diminished highly when cultured together. P. aurelia grew better when grown alone since, when cultured with P. caudata it also faced some competition, that transitioned to a lack of food resources. It is improbable that P. aurelia acts as a parasite since the species are similar. Therefore, the correct answer is option B.

questions.text: A certain group of pine trees is observed to have a mutation that causes proteins to be retained in the cell instead of being secreted. This may likely indicate a problem with which of the following organelles?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribosomes will not have any problem if the proteins are not being secreted.
Lysosomes have no role in protein synthesis.

This is actually a fairly basic question regarding cellular organelles, but it’s presented in a tricky way. Ribosomes are the site of protein synthesis in the cell, and ribosomes attached to the rough ER synthesize proteins that will either end up in the cell membrane or be secreted. From the rough ER, these proteins travel to the Golgi apparatus for sorting and packaging, and ultimately, secretion from the cell. Thus, if the proteins are not being secreted, it is most likely a problem with the Golgi apparatus since the Golgi apparatus, using secretory vesicles as a means of transport, secretes these proteins out of the cell.
Microtubules have no role in protein synthesis.
Mitochondria has no role in protein synthesis.

questions.text: Match the columns:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: The stomach starts the chemical digestion of protein. The enzyme pepsin is responsible for protein breakdown. questions.text: A community includes four different populations with various degrees of interbreeding. Population 1 shares genes only with Population 2. Population 2 shares genes with Populations 1 and 4. Population 3 has no gene flow with other populations. Population 4 shares genes only with Population 2. Which population(s) is (are) most likely to evolve after a change in its environment?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Remember to take the question apart piece by piece—don’t guess until you are sure what the question is asking! The best way to tackle this question is to draw a diagram of the interactions of the populations with one another. It might look something like the diagram below. The question goes on to ask which population is most likely to evolve after a change—in other words, which population can best adapt to its new environment. You should know that evolution requires genetic variability, which is achieved through random mutation and exchange of genetic material with other organisms (sexual reproduction). The population that will be most likely to evolve is the one in which there is the greatest genetic variability. In this situation, that population is Population 2 because it interacts with most other populations.

questions.text: If we were to continue adding monomers to this molecule to increase its size, to which atom would the next portion be bonded?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: When a new amino acid is added to a protein, it is added to the carbon atom on the far right of the molecule. The OH group already attached to this carbon is lost as part of a water molecule. The answer can also be 1, however we are restricted to follow the orientation, provided to us, which is addition happens on the C-terminal whereas the N-terminals remains stable.

questions.text: A cell at telophase stage is observed by a student in a plant brought from the field. He tells his teacher that this cell is not like other cells at telophase stage. There is no formation of cell plate and thus the cell is containing more number of chromosomes as compared to other dividing cells. This would result in:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Polyploids arise when a rare mitotic or meiotic catastrophe, such as nondisjunction, causes the formation of gametes that have a complete set of duplicate chromosomes. Diploid gametes are frequently formed in this way. When a diploid gamete fuses with a haploid gamete, a triploid zygote forms, although these triploids are generally unstable and can often be sterile. Many types of polyploids are found in nature, including tetraploids (four sets of chromosomes), hexaploids (six sets of chromosomes), and other chromosome-pair multiples. Polyploidy may occur due to abnormal cell division, either during mitosis, or commonly during metaphase I in meiosis.

questions.text: In the lungs, oxygen and carbon dioxide pass through cell membranes by diffusion. Which row is correct?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Oxygen goes from the alveolar's lumen to the cytoplasm of the erythrocyte, and that's 5 membranes: the "top" of the alveolar epithelial cell, the "bottom" of such cell, the "top" of the endothelial cell (capillary), the "bottom" of such cell, the erythrocyte membrane. You got all the cells right, but your only problem was this: oxygen, diffuses through the cell membrane, enters the cell, moves through the cytoplasm, and diffuses through the membrane again, exiting the cell. So, for each cell, you have to count 2 membranes. For the last one, the erythrocyte, you have only 1 membrane (because it is O2's final destination). For the CO2, the situation is a little bit more tricky. We have the same 4 membranes (2x epithelial cell and 2x capillary), but CO2 can come from 2 locations: From the erythrocyte, where it is formed from H2CO3 (by the reaction H2CO3 → H2O + CO2) or released from the N-terminal group of proteins, like haemoglobin (where it has previously bound). From the plasma (around 9% of the CO2). In the first case, we have 5 membranes and in the second case, just 4. So, the correct answer is D.

questions.text: Ingestion of the insecticide parathion, which blocks acetylcholinesterase function, would cause:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: A halt to all synaptic nervous transmissions
An increase in acetylcholine concentration in synapses
A decrease in acetylcholine concentration in synapses
Levels of acetylcholine to remain the same

questions.explanation: Acetylcholine is a neurotransmitter, which causes depolarization of the postsynaptic membrane of one neuron when released by the presynaptic terminal of another neuron. Acetylcholine is removed from the synapse by the enzyme acetylcholinesterase. Anticholinesterase inhibitors like parathion disrupt this activity, meaning that the acetylcholine can’t be degraded and the concentration of acetylcholine in the synapse will increase.

questions.text: Ahmad has a swollen neck. He is losing his weight from last few months drastically. When checked, the heart rate also seems to be increased. He claims that he is not able to sleep properly and he feels restless. What can be the probable diagnosis of this situation?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Hyperthyroidism (overactive thyroid) occurs when your thyroid gland produces too much of the hormone, thyroxine. Hyperthyroidism, as the same suggests, is the hyperplasia of the thyroid, causing it to expand, thus the swollen neck. Hyperthyroidism can accelerate your body's metabolism, causing unintentional weight loss and a rapid or irregular heartbeat. Hyperthyroidism (overactive) can cause difficulty sleeping due to arousals from nervousness or irritability, as well as muscle weakness and constant feelings of tiredness.

questions.text: A stable population exists in Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium with two alleles, T and t. TT and Tt individuals have the ability to curl their tongues, while tt individuals cannot curl their tongues. The allele frequency of T is 0.8. What is the percentage of individuals that can curl their tongues?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.explanation: Individuals that can curl their tongues have at least one dominant allele. This means that you are looking for the percentage of p2 individuals, who have two dominant alleles, and the percentage of 2pq individuals, who have only one dominant allele. These two percentages added together will give you the percentage of individuals that can curl their tongues. Therefore, (0.8)2 + 2(0.8)(0.2) = 0.96 or 96%.

questions.text: The correct order of hydration energy is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Hydration energy (also hydration enthalpy) is the amount of energy released when one mole of ions undergoes hydration. Hydration energy is one component in the quantitative analysis of solvation. The size of the hydration enthalpy is governed by the amount of attraction between the ions and the water molecules. The stronger the attraction, the smaller the ion is. For example, hydration enthalpy falls as you go down a group, in the Periodic Table. Moreover, the stronger the attraction, the more highly charged the ion is. Furthermore, attractions are stronger in ions, with higher charge densities (small ion, greater oxidation number).

questions.text: Which of the following is not a colligative property?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: In chemistry, colligative properties are those properties of solutions that depend on the ratio of the number of solute particles to the number of solvent molecules in a solution, and not on the nature of the chemical species present. Colligative properties include vapor pressure lowering, boiling point elevation, freezing point depression, and osmotic pressure.

questions.text: The Van't Hoff's factor ′i′ accounts for:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The Van't Hoff's factor 'i' accounts for the extent of dissociation of solute. For example, one molecule of NaCl completely dissociates to produce two ions. Hence, Van't Hoff's factor 'i' is 2. One molecule of Na2SO4 completely dissociates to produce three ions. Hence, Van't Hoff's factor 'i' is 3.

questions.text: The half-life period of C-14 is 5760 years. A 100mg sample of C-14 will be reduced to 25mg in:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Since two half-lives have passed for a 100 mg sample to be reduced into 25 mg (100 mg to 50 mg and 50 mg to 25 mg) Total time = 2 half-lives Total time = 2 x 57,60 years Total time = 11,520 years

questions.text: Choose the best reducing agent among the following:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Fluorine is the most electronegative element, so forming F- is the most energetically favorable of the standard reductions. This makes fluorine the most powerful oxidizing agent – it will even react with water, it's that strong! Chlorine has the ability to take electrons from both bromide ions and iodide ions. Iodine, being the weakest oxidizing agent, is the strongest reducing agent, since it is the least electronegative amongst all of the halogens. It cannot take an electron from any other in the group; however, I- is perfectly capable of giving an electron.

questions.text: One of the methods used for the preparation of ethers is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The Williamson ether synthesis occurs by an SN2 reaction in which a metal alkoxide displaces a halide ion from an alkyl halide. The alkoxide ion is prepared by the reaction of an alcohol with a strong base such as sodium hydride.The Cannizzaro reaction is when a non-enolizable aldehyde reacts with itself in a strong base, such as sodium hydroxide (NaOH), to form a carboxylic acid and an alcohol. A non-enolizable aldehyde is one that has no alpha hydrogens available for the aldehyde to form an enol.In the Wurtz reaction, two alkyl halides are reacted with sodium metal in a dry ether solution to form a higher alkane.The preparation of dialkyl zinc from zinc and alkyl iodide is called the Frankland reaction.

questions.text: Solvent extraction is an equilibrium process and is controlled by:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Solvent extraction is the process in which a compound transfers from one solvent to another owing to the difference in solubility or distribution coefficient between these two immiscible (or slightly soluble) solvents. Solvent extraction is an equilibrium process controlled by distribution law or partition law. It states that a solute distributes itself between two immiscible liquids in a constant ratio of concentrations irrespective of the amount of solute added.Law of mass action is a law stating that the rate of any chemical reaction is proportional to the product of the masses of the reacting substances, with each mass raised to a power equal to the coefficient that occurs in the chemical equation.

questions.text: Which of the following properties depends upon the amount of matter present in the system?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Gibbs free energy is an extensive property that depends upon the amount of substance present in the system. If the matter in a given system at a given condition is divided into two equal parts, the values of the extensive properties will become half of the original values. Other extensive properties include mass, volume, moles, enthalpy, entropy, and internal energy.

questions.text: The number of electrons present in n = 2, l = 1 and m = -1, 0, +1 are:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: n is the principal quantum number and indicates the number of shells. n = 2 indicates that the second shell is being discussed. l is the azimuthal quantum number and indicates the number of subshells. l = 0 means s subshell, l = 1 means p subshell, and so on. m is the magnetic quantum number and indicates the orientation of orbitals in the subshells. m ranges from -1 to +1 through 0. Since m = -1, 0, +1, this means that all three sub-orbitals; 2px, 2py, 2pz are taken into consideration. Since all of these have 2 electrons each, the total number of electrons in them is 6.

questions.text: The chemical name of [Zn(OH)4]2- is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Ligands are named first in alphabetical order. The name of the metal comes next. The oxidation state of the metal follows, noted by a Roman numeral in parentheses (II, IV). Ligands that act as anions that end in "-ide" are replaced with an ending "-o" (e.g., Hydroxide → Hydroxo). The number of ligands present in the complex is indicated with the prefixes di, tri, etc. Prefixes always go before the ligand name; they are not taken into account when putting ligands in alphabetical order. When naming the metal center, you must know the formal metal name and the oxidation state. To show the oxidation state, we use Roman numerals inside parenthesis. If the overall coordination complex is an anion, the ending "-ate" is attached to the metal center.

questions.text: Oxidation state of ‘Mn’ in KMnO4, K2MnO4, MnO2 and MnSO4 is in the order:
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.text: The correct order of boiling points of the given liquid is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: H2O has a higher boiling point than HF because it contains twice as many Hydrogen bonds, despite these being individually weaker. The intermolecular bonding for HF is hydrogen, whereas, for HCl, the intermolecular bonding is Permanent Dipole. Since hydrogen bonding is stronger than permanent dipoles, HF will have a higher boiling temperature. Due to the relatively high electronegativity of nitrogen than chlorine, N-----H is stronger than H-----Cl and thus NH3 has higher boiling point than HCl.

questions.text: Relative acidic strength of alcohol, phenol, water and carboxylic acid is:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: In the case of phenols, the negative charge is less effectively delocalized over one oxygen atom and less electronegative carbon atoms in phenoxide ions. Carboxylic acids are more acidic than phenols because resonance delocalization of charge stabilizes the conjugate base anion carboxylate relative to the reactant acid. Therefore, the carboxylate ion exhibits higher stability in comparison to the phenoxide ion. Hence, carboxylic acids are more acidic than phenols.Phenols are much more acidic than alcohols because the negative charge in the phenoxide ion is not localized on the oxygen atom, as it is in an alkoxide ion, but is delocalized-it is shared by a number of carbon atoms in the benzene ring.Larger substituents as in alcohol are better electron donors, which destabilize the resulting alkoxide anions. Because hydrogen is the least donating of the substituents, water is the stronger acid than alcohol.

questions.text: In an electrolytic cell, a salt bridge is used in order to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Allow movement of ions between two half-cells

questions.explanation: A salt bridge is used to connect the oxidation and reduction half-cells of a galvanic cell (voltaic cell), a type of electrochemical cell. It maintains electrical neutrality within the internal circuit, preventing the cell from rapidly running its reaction to equilibrium. The cations, positive ions, flow toward the cathode to replace the cations that are being picked up at the electrode. The anions, negative ions, flow toward the anode to balance the positive charge of the cations that are released from the electrode.

questions.text: Stronger the oxidizing agent, greater is the:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: A stronger oxidizing agent means it undergoes reduction more easily and thus has greater standard reduction potential. Reduction potential (Eo) is defined as a tendency of a chemical species to be reduced by gaining an electron and is defined with electrochemical reference of hydrogen, which is globally given the reduction potential of zero.

questions.text: The mobile phase in paper chromatography is usually:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: A paper chromatography variant, two-dimensional chromatography involves using two solvents and rotating the paper 90° in between. The mobile phase is generally a mixture of non-polar organic solvent, while the stationary phase is polar inorganic solvent water.

questions.text: What is the most specific characterization of the reaction shown? Ca(OH)2 (aq) + H2SO4 (aq) → CaSO4 (aq) + H2O (l)
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: This reaction is a classic example of a neutralization reaction, in which an acid and a base react to form a salt and, usually, water. Although this reaction also fits the criteria for a double-displacement reaction, choice (C), in which two molecules essentially exchange ions with each other, neutralization is a more specific description of the process.

questions.text: SN1 reactions show first-order kinetics because:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: The rate-limiting step is the first step to occur in the reaction
The rate-limiting step involves only one molecule
There is only one rate-limiting step
The reaction involves only one molecule

questions.explanation: An SN1 reaction is a first-order nucleophilic substitution reaction. It is called first-order because the rate-limiting step involves only one molecule. Choice (A) is true, but does not explain why SN1 reactions have first-order kinetics; the rate-limiting step of an SN2 reaction is also the first (and only) step of that reaction, but SN2 reactions have second-order kinetics, not first-order. This is because two reactants are involved in the rate-limiting step, both influencing the rate of reaction. Choice (C) is a true statement as well, but again, does not explain why the reaction is first-order. Finally, choice (D) is incorrect because it is the rate-limiting step, not the overall reaction, that involves only one molecule.

questions.text: (1): If a body, moving in a circular path has a constant speed, then there is no force acting on it. (2): The direction of the velocity vector of a body, moving in a circular path, is changing.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Both (1) and (2) are true and (2) is the correct explanation of (1)
Both (1) and (2) are true but (2) is not the correct explanation of (1)

questions.explanation: (A) For a body moving in a circular path at a constant speed, the centripetal force is always present on the body, acting towards the center. (B) Direction of the velocity vector of a body, moving in a circular path, always keeps on changing or the position vector of the particle keeps on changing.

questions.text: A satellite is currently orbiting Earth in a circular orbit of radius R; its kinetic energy is K. If the satellite is moved and enters a new circular orbit of radius 2R, what will be it's kinetic energy?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The gravitational pull by the Earth provides the centripetal force on the satellite, so GMm/R2=mv2/R. The kinetic energy, K, of the satellite is inversely proportional to R. Therefore, if R increases by a factor of 2, then K decreases by a factor of 2.

questions.text: Tungsten-176 has a half-life of 2.5 hours. After how many hours will the disintegration rate of a tungsten-176 sample drop to 1/10 of its initial value?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: After 3 half-lives, the activity will drop to (½)3 = 1/8 its initial value, and after 4 half-lives, it will drop to (½)4 = 1/16 its initial value. Since 1/10 is between ⅛ and 1/16, the time interval in this case is between 3 and 4 half-lives, that is, between 3(2.5 h) = 7.5 h and 4(2.5 h) = 10 h. Only (B) is in this range.

questions.text: In Young's Double-Slit interference experiment, what is the difference in path length of the light waves from the two slits at the center of the first bright fringe above the central maximum?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Since the fringe is bright, the waves must interfere constructively. This implies that the difference in path lengths must be a whole number times the wavelength, eliminating (B),(C), and (E). The central maximum is equidistant from the two slits, so the change in length is zero at the center. At the first bright fringe. Above the central maximum, we have change in length equal to lambda.

questions.text: As a particle moves in from infinity, the value of Absolute Gravitational Potential energy:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: As infinity is undefined, we assume that particles that are at infinity have zero Absolute Gravitational Potential Energy. Any object moving away from infinity has a decrease in Absolute Gravitational Potential Energy.

questions.text: If a bus collides with a fire hydrant, what will be the magnitude of the force experienced by the fire hydrant?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Much greater than the bus experienced by the fire hydrant
Much lesser than the bus experienced by the fire hydrant

10 times less than the bus experienced by the fire hydrant

questions.explanation: Newton's third law states: For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. The magnitude of the forces on the first object equals the magnitude of the force on the second object, and is not affected by the mass of either object.

questions.text: Charges of +2uC, point P, and -2uC, point Q, are 2 meters away from each other. X is a point at the midpoint of both these charges.Which of the following correctly describes the electric field and the electric potential at point X?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Remember Electric Field always moves from positive charge to negative charge, hence here it will direct from P to Q. Electric Potential can be added or subtracted, here since the charges are of opposite signs and same magnitude, we will subtract and hence electric potential will be zero.

questions.text: The time of one vibration of a simple pendulum may be decreased by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Increasing the length of the pendulum
Decreasing the length of the pendulum

questions.explanation: The formula for the time period of a pendulum is : T=2 (π) √(L/G). As time has a direct relation with length, decreasing length will result in a decrease of the time period. It must be remembered that G in the formula represents gravity and mass does not have any effect on the time period.

questions.text: A car going around a certain curve at a speed of 25 km/h has centripetal force of 100 N acting on it. If the speed of the car is doubled, the centripetal force:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Is reduced to 1/2 of the original value
Is reduced to 1/6 of the original value

questions.explanation: The formula that will be used here will be: F=(m)(v)2/r. By doubling the speed of the car, the centripetal force should be quadrupled due to the power of 2.

questions.text: A train is leaving a station at 90 km/h, sounding a whistle of frequency 1000 Hz. What will be the apparent frequency heard by the listener sitting on the platform if the train moves away from the station? (speed of sound = 340 m/s).
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: f' = [(v - vo)/(v + vs)] x f where; f'= observed frequency, f= actual frequency, v= velocity of the sound waves, vo= velocity of the observer, vs= velocity of the source.We will first have to convert 90 km/h into m/s= 90 x (1000/3600). The answer comes as 25 m/s. f'= [(340 - 0)/(340 + 25)] x 1000 Hz; where vo is 0 m/s, since the observer is not moving, rather is stationary at the station. f'= 931. Hz

questions.text: A conductor carrying a current 'I' has length 'L'. When it is placed in a magnetic field 'B' at 90°, it experiences a force:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The formula for the force experienced by a current-carrying conductor is BILsin(θ), where theta represents the angle between the current, I, and the magnetic field, B, hence sin(90)=1 and the value of the force will be BIL (maximum).

questions.text: A sphere of charge +Q is fixed in a position. A smaller sphere of charge +q is placed near the larger sphere and released from rest. The small sphere will move away from the large sphere with:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: When the smaller charge is placed, it undergoes repulsion as ‘like’ charges repel. Since it was at zero velocity, the velocity increases. But as the charge moves further away, the force of repulsion decreases, and according to the formula, F= kQq/r2, acceleration decreases.

questions.text: In a step-up transformer, the current in the secondary coil is _ than the current in the primary coil.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Can’t be determined from the given information.

questions.explanation: A step-up transformer turns low-voltage electricity into high-voltage electricity while dropping the current. The relationship to remember is Vs/Vp= Ip/Is= Ns/Np. Following this relationship, we can determine that the Current will be lesser in the Secondary Coil.

questions.text: A car starts from rest and moves with a constant acceleration. During the 5th second of its motion, it covers a distance of 36 meters. What is the acceleration of the car?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: You will find the distance traveled in 5 and 4 seconds individually. After subtracting these two terms and equating it to 36 meters, all variables are fulfilled other than a which we can solve for. In the below equations, s is distance, a is acceleration, and t is time. In 4 seconds, the truck covers: s = 1/2 at2 s = 1/2 (a) 42 s = 1/2 (a) (16) s = 8 (a) meters In 5 seconds, the truck covers s = 1/2 a t2 s = 1/2 (a) 52 s = 1/2 (a) 25 s = 12.5 (a) meters The distance covered in 5 seconds minus the distance covered in 4 seconds is the distance (36 meters) covered in the 5th second. 36 = 12.5 a - 8 a 36 meters = 4.5 seconds2 (a) a = acceleration = 8 m/s2

questions.text: The torque will be greater if:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Both magnitude of force and moment arm are smaller
Both magnitude of force and moment arm are greater
Only magnitude of force is greater

questions.explanation: The formula for torque is T=Fr. Hence Torque will be greater if the magnitude of the force and moment arm are greater.

questions.text: A rescue helicopter drops a package of emergency ration to a stranded party on the ground. If the helicopter is travelling horizontally at 40 m/s at a height of 100m above the ground, where does the package strike the ground relative to the point it was released? g=9.8m/s2.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The first thing we have to figure out is the Initial Vertical Speed of the package. The helicopter speed is only the horizontal component so we can ignore that. Initial Vertical speed will hence be 0. Using the formula: Distance = (Initial Vertical Speed x time) + 1/2gt² 100 = 1/2 (9.8) t² t² = 100×2/9.8 t² = 100/4.9 t² = 20.4 t = 4.5 Horizontal Distance = Horizontal Speed x Time X = 40×4.5 X = 180.7

questions.text: Assume that you have two balls of identical volume, one weighing 2 Newtons and the other 10 Newtons. Both are falling after being released from the same point simultaneously. Which of the following will then be true: I) The 10 Newton ball falling freely from rest will be accelerated at a greater rate than the 2 Newton ball II) At the end of 4 seconds of freefall, the 10 Newton ball will have five times the momentum of the 2 Newton ball III) At the end of 4 seconds of the fall the 10 Newton ball will have twice the kinetic energy as of the 2 Newton ball IV) The 10 Newton ball possesses more inertia compared to the 2 Newton ball
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: If the balls are falling in free fall, then they will encounter no air resistance. Hence they will only undergo acceleration due to gravity, which will be equal on both. As Momentum is dependent on both Mass and Velocity, due to the higher mass the 10 Newton Ball will have a higher momentum. KE stays constant. Since the velocities are constant at all times, the ratio of KE, between both the balls, would be constant as well; KE= 1/2 mv2 1/2 x 2v2 : 1/2 x 10v2 2:10 ---> 1:5

questions.text: If the pressure is increased, the melting point of ice:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The melting point of ice decreases when pressure increases because when pressure is increased, volume is decreased and the volume of water is less than ice. To remember: When skaters skate on ice, the increased pressure due to their weight causes ice to melt and allows them to move smoothly. When the pressure is removed, the water again freezes. However, on a contrary, the boiling point increases as the pressure increases.

questions.text: A nonconducting sphere is given a non zero net electric charge,+Q, and then brought close to a neutral conducting sphere of the same radius. Which of the following will be true?

The spheres will experience an electrostatic repulsion
The spheres will experience no electrostatic interaction

questions.explanation: The proximity of the charged sphere will induce negative charges to move to the side of the uncharged sphere closer to the charged sphere. Since the induced negative charge is closer than the induced positive charge to the charged sphere, there will be a net electrostatic attraction between the spheres.

questions.text: What will be the value of gravity if you go a distance equal to the diameter of the Earth from its surface?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The value of g at a height is given by the equation: g=GM/(R+h)2. Where G is the universal constant of gravitation, M is the mass of the Earth, R is the radius of earth and h is the height of an object above the surface of Earth. As the height here is equal to 2 Earth Radius so the equation would be: g=GM/(R+2R)2 That would be g/9.

questions.text: Which two of the following statements about genetic variation are correct?1. Asexual reproduction always produces genetic variation. 2. Gamete production is the only possible source of genetic variation in sexual reproduction. 3. The environment can cause genetic variation. 4. Mutations can produce genetic variation.
questions.subject: Science Reasoning
questions.explanation: Asexual reproduction produces genetically identical offspring so it does not usually result in genetic variation. If a mutation occurs, it would result in genetic variation. Statement 1 is therefore not correct.Gamete production does result in genetic variation within sexual reproduction. However, it is not the only source of genetic variation: for example, the randomization of which sperm fertilizes the egg brings about genetic variation. Statement 2 is, therefore, not correct.The environment can cause genetic variation: for example, carcinogens in cigarette smoke can cause genetic mutations that lead to genetic variation. Statement 3 is therefore correct. A mutation affects the DNA sequence. A change in the DNA results in genetic variation. Statement 4 is therefore correct.

questions.text: Hot, concentrated aqueous sodium hydroxide and chlorine react as shown in the equation: 6NaOH + 3Cl2 → 5NaCl+ NaClO3 + 3H2O Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. Chlorine has an oxidation state of +5 in NaClO3. 2. This is an example of a disproportionation reaction. 3. Some of the oxygen in the hydroxide ions is oxidised.
questions.subject: Science Reasoning
questions.explanation: The chlorine in the reactants is in oxidation state 0. Two of the products contain chlorine. In NaCl, the chlorine is in oxidation state –1 and has therefore been reduced. In NaClO3, the chlorine is in oxidation state +5 and has therefore been oxidised. Statement 1 is correct. The reaction is a redox reaction. Cl2 has been both reduced and oxidised, and so it is a disproportionation reaction. Statement 2 is correct. The oxygen atoms in the hydroxide ions are in oxidation state –2. They do not change oxidation state and remain in the oxidation state –2 in the water molecule. Statement 3 is not correct.

questions.text: The reaction between hydrogen and nitrogen in the presence of an iron catalyst is known as the Haber process. It is a reversible reaction. The energy profile for this reaction is shown. What is the energy change when one mole of ammonia is decomposed into its component elements?
questions.subject: Science Reasoning
questions.explanation: In the forward direction, the energy change is determined by the relative energy of the reactants and the products. The energy change for the formation of two moles of ammonia is shown as –92 kJ. This means that the energy change for the formation of one mole of ammonia is –46 kJ. The negative sign indicates that energy is released during the formation (an exothermic reaction). The decomposition of one mole of ammonia is the reverse process for the formation, and so the energy change is +46 kJ. The positive sign indicates that the energy is absorbed (an endothermic reaction).

Combustion, the activity of decomposers, and aerobic respiration in both plants and animals add carbon dioxide to the atmosphere. These are in options A, C, D and F. The only process to remove carbon dioxide is photosynthesis. This is in option C and D. The only process to add oxygen to the atmosphere is photosynthesis. This is in options A and D. The activity of decomposers, combustion and aerobic respiration all remove oxygen from the atmosphere, so all of the options in the final column are correct. The only row that has all columns correct is option D.
Since row D is the correct answer, hence this option is incorrect.
Since row D is the correct answer, hence this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Combustion, the activity of decomposers, and aerobic respiration in both plants and animals add carbon dioxide to the atmosphere. These are in options A, C, D and F. The only process to remove carbon dioxide is photosynthesis. This is in option C and D. The only process to add oxygen to the atmosphere is photosynthesis. This is in options A and D. The activity of decomposers, combustion and aerobic respiration all remove oxygen from the atmosphere, so all of the options in the final column are correct. The only row that has all columns correct is option D.

questions.text: Six test tubes were set up. Two different solutions were added to each test tube as shown in the table. The temperature, pH, and all concentrations were optimal. Which three mixtures would lead to the presence of amino acids in the test tube?
questions.subject: Science Reasoning
questions.explanation: In order to produce amino acids, a protease enzyme needs to be mixed with a protein substrate. In test tube 1, the lipase will break down the lipids into fatty acids. This will not result in any amino acids being present. In test tube 2, the lipase will not have any effect on the boiled protein. This will not result in any amino acids being present. In test tube 3, the protease will break down the lipase. Although lipase is an enzyme, it is also a protein, so in this case it is also a substrate for the protease. This will result in amino acids being present. In test tube 4, the protease has been boiled, and this has caused it to become denatured. A denatured enzyme does not break down a substrate, even if it is present. Therefore amino acids will not be present. In test tube 5, the protease will break down the boiled protein into amino acids. Boiling a substrate does not prevent it from being broken down by an appropriate enzyme. This will result in amino acids being present. In test tube 6, the protease will break down the protein into amino acids. This will result in amino acids being present.

questions.text: The compounds BrCl and BrI melt at –66°C and 42°C, respectively.What is the best explanation for this difference in melting points?
questions.subject: Science Reasoning

options.text: The Br-I covalent bond is stronger than the Br−Cl covalent bond
Chlorine is more reactive than iodine so chlorine breaks away from bromine more readily than iodine
The forces between BrI molecules are stronger than the forces between BrCl molecules
Iodide ions are larger than chloride ions so bromide ions form stronger ionic bonds with iodide ions than with chloride ions
Iodine atoms are larger than chlorine atoms so the BrI giant covalent lattice is more difficult to break up than the BrCl giant covalent lattice

questions.explanation: Chlorine, bromine, and iodine are elements in Group 17 (the halogens). As typical non-metals, they form simple covalently bonded molecules. So options D and E are not correct. When simple molecular compounds melt it is the forces between the molecules that are overcome, not the covalent bonds within the molecules. So options A and B are not correct. Atomic radius increases down a Group as more electron shells are filled, so iodine atoms are much larger than chlorine atoms. This means that BrI molecules are larger than BrCl molecules. Therefore the temporary dipole–induced dipole interactions would be greater between BrI molecules as these molecules have more electrons and a larger surface area than BrCl molecules. So option C is correct.

questions.text: A bar magnet is rotating at a constant rate of 120 revolutions per minute inside a circular coil of wire, causing an induced voltage across the coil. Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. Increasing the number of turns of wire on the coil would increase both the maximum value and the frequency of the induced voltage. 2. The direction of the induced voltage in this coil reverses every 0.25 seconds. 3.There is always an induced current in a conductor which is experiencing a change in magnetic field.
questions.subject: Science Reasoning

options.explanation: Since statements 2 and 3 are correct, hence this option is incorrect.
Since statements 2 and 3 are correct, hence this option is incorrect.
Since statements 2 and 3 are correct, hence this option is incorrect.
Since statements 2 and 3 are correct, hence this option is incorrect.
Since statements 2 and 3 are correct, hence this option is incorrect.

Let's analyze each statement: Increasing the number of turns of wire on the coil would increase both the maximum value and the frequency of the induced voltage. This statement is partially correct. Increasing the number of turns of wire on the coil will increase the maximum value of the induced voltage. This is because a higher number of turns will result in a stronger magnetic flux linkage between the coil and the rotating bar magnet, leading to a larger induced voltage. However, the frequency of the induced voltage will not change by increasing the number of turns. The frequency of the induced voltage is determined solely by the rotation rate of the magnet and remains constant. In this case, since the bar magnet is rotating at a constant rate of 120 revolutions per minute, the frequency of the induced voltage will also be constant. The direction of the induced voltage in this coil reverses every 0.25 seconds. This statement is correct. The induced voltage in the coil will change its direction every half revolution of the magnet. Since the magnet is rotating at a constant rate of 120 revolutions per minute, the time taken for half a revolution (180 degrees) is 1/2 of a full rotation period. To calculate the time taken for half a revolution, we can use: Time for half revolution = 1 / (2 * frequency) Time for half revolution = 1 / (2 * 120/60) = 0.25 seconds Therefore, the direction of the induced voltage will reverse every 0.25 seconds. There is always an induced current in a conductor experiencing a change in magnetic field. This statement is correct. According to Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction, when a conductor (or a coil of wire) experiences a change in magnetic field, an induced electromotive force (EMF) or voltage is produced across the conductor or coil. This induced voltage can lead to the flow of an induced current if the circuit is closed (i.e., if the conductor forms a complete loop). In the scenario described, as the bar magnet rotates inside the circular coil of wire, the magnetic field passing through the coil changes, leading to an induced voltage across the coil. If the coil is part of a closed circuit, it will give rise to an induced current in the circuit. The direction of the induced current can be determined using Lenz's law, which states that the induced current flows in a direction that opposes the change in magnetic flux causing it.

questions.explanation: Statements 2 and 3 are correct.

questions.text: A submarine uses sound of frequency 6000 Hz to locate obstructions ahead of it in the sea. A pulse of this sound reflects off an object that is 750m in front of the submarine. The pulse returns to the submarine 1.00s after it is transmitted. What is the wavelength of this sound in the sea water?
questions.subject: Science Reasoning
questions.explanation: The sound travels to the obstruction and reflects back. This is a distance of 2×750m = 1500m The time taken is 1.00s and so the speed of the sound in seawater is 1500/1.00= 1500ms-1 The frequency is 6000 Hz wavelength = wave speed/frequency= 1500/6000= 0.250m = 25.0cm

questions.text: A volunteer was injected intravenously with several test substances to determine the effect of each substance on normal body variables. The results are shown in Table 1. Assume that enough time was allowed between injections so that the substances do not interfere with one another. Based on the information in Table 1, which of the following is most likely substance B?

questions.explanation: From Table 1, substance B caused an increase in serum calcium levels, which is the effect that parathyroid hormone has on the body.

questions.text: A volunteer was injected intravenously with several test substances to determine the effect of each substance on normal body variables. The results are shown in Table 1. Under what conditions might substance D be released normally?
questions.subject: Science Reasoning

options.text: When there has been limited intake of dietary calcium
When dietary calcium is in excess

questions.explanation: Substance D causes an increase in serum sodium, which is the effect aldosterone has on the body. Aldosterone is released when blood pressure is low because excess sodium will have the effect of causing water retention, which will increase blood volume, which will increase blood pressure. (Even if you did not know this, you should have been able to eliminate the other choices.)

questions.text: Three different cell types were observed under the microscope. The observations are summarized in Table 1. The three cell types were grown in separate cultures with plenty of oxygen and nutrients available. Figure 1 shows their rates of growth. At Time 1, oxygen was no longer available to the cells. Which of the following equations is cell Type C able to carry out? I. Glucose + Oxygen → Carbon Dioxide + Water + ATP II. Water + Light → Oxygen + ATP + NADPH III. Carbon Dioxide + Water + ATP + NADPH → Glucose
questions.subject: Science Reasoning
questions.explanation: Cell Type C has a nucleus, a cell wall, and chloroplasts and therefore most likely comes from a plant. Equations II and III are the equations for photosynthesis and would occur in plants, so you can eliminate (A), (B), and (C). Equation I is the equation for cellular respiration, which also occurs in plants, so (D) can be eliminated.

questions.text: Three different cell types were observed under the microscope. The observations are summarized in Table 1. The three cell types were grown in separate cultures with plenty of oxygen and nutrients available. Figure 1 shows their rates of growth. At Time 1, oxygen was no longer available to the cells. Consider Figure 1. Which of the following statements best describes the reason for the difference between the curves for cell Type B and cell Type C?
questions.subject: Science Reasoning

options.text: Cell Type B is unable to survive in the presence of oxygen,while cell Type C can ferment
The products of fermentation in cell Type C are toxic to the cells and they are dying
Cell Type B is an obligate aerobe, while cell Type C is able to ferment
Cell Type B is a facultative anaerobe, while cell Type C is an obligate aerobe
Cell Type C is an obligate aerobe, while cell Type B is an obligate anaerobe

questions.explanation: This is a great question to do some answer predicting on. At Time 1, the oxygen was removed from the cultures, and cell Type C died. Clearly, it is an obligate aerobe. Thus, you can eliminate (A), (B), and (C). Because cell Type B was growing well in the presence of oxygen, it cannot be an obligate anaerobe, thus (E) can be eliminated. Cell Type B must be a facultative anaerobe, using oxygen when it is available and fermentation when oxygen is not available. The decrease in growth of cell Type B after Time 1 is most likely because less energy is produced during fermentation than during aerobic metabolism.

questions.text: Unfertilized frog oocytes were bathed in a neutral, isotonic solution (Frog Ringer’s solution) to prevent changes in volume due to osmosis. The Frog Ringer’s solution was supplemented with radiolabeled amino acids. A sample of oocytes was taken every 30 minutes and assayed for radioactivity. At Time 1, the oocytes were fertilized. Samples were taken at five-minute intervals after fertilization and assayed for radioactivity. The results are presented in the graph below. Based on the results of the experiment, the process most likely occurring after fertilization is
questions.subject: Science Reasoning
questions.explanation: Of the choices available, you should be able to eliminate (E), endocytosis, first. There is no evidence to support this. As for the remaining choices, be careful not to come to conclusions too quickly based on your prior knowledge. In other words, you know that after fertilization occurs the zygote undergoes rapid cell division. This means that DNA will be replicated, RNA will be transcribed, proteins will be synthesized, and ultimately, organogenesis will occur. So, in effect, (A), (B), (C), and (D) are all occurring. The question, however, asks specifically for the process that is most likely occurring based on the results of the experiment. Because the experiment measures the amount of a radiolabeled amino acid present in the cells after fertilization, it is most directly measuring the rate of protein synthesis, making (C) the best answer.

questions.text: What is the most advantageous difference between the ancestral primate skulls and the modern human skull?
questions.subject: Science Reasoning
questions.explanation: Having forward-facing eyes, (A), was certainly an advantage as it increased visual depth perception. However, because all the skulls have forward-facing eyes, this does not represent a difference between ancient primates and modern humans. Loss of the canine teeth, loss of the brow ridge, and a reduction in jaw size—(C), (D), and (E)—are also apparent differences, but none is as helpful as the increased brain capacity.

questions.text: Evolution of a bacterial population occurs much more rapidly than evolution of a human population. This is because:
questions.subject: Science Reasoning

options.text: The bacteria are smaller and thus more susceptible to change
The bacterial life cycle is short and many new generation scan be produced quickly
Humans can only reproduce during a portion of their lifecycle, whereas bacteria can reproduce throughout their entire life cycle
Bacteria do not require oxygen to survive

questions.explanation: Evolution will always occur faster in an organism that has short generation times simply because it is replicating its DNA more frequently and therefore has a greater chance of mutations occurring.

questions.text: During tracheotomy (a tube is inserted directly into the trachea), colder and drier air than normal enters the lungs which can cause lung crusting and infection. The primary cause for this is:
questions.subject: Science Reasoning

options.text: Air enters the respiratory system too rapidly to be filtered
Air is not properly humidified by the larynx
Air does not flow through the nasal passageways
Air does not flow past the mouth and tongue

questions.explanation: In a tracheotomy, the tube is inserted through a cut in the neck below the vocal cords this allows air to enter the lungs. Breathing is then done through the tube, bypassing the mouth, nose, and throat. Air from your nose and mouth passes down through your throat to the larynx and into the trachea. The trachea is the big air tube leading to the lungs. When you breathe out, air leaves your lungs through this tube. Hence, During tracheotomy (a tube inserted directly into the trachea), colder and drier air enters the lungs than normal which can cause lung crusting and infection. The primary cause for this is air does not flow through the nasal passageways. So, the correct answer is 'Air does not flow through the nasal passageways'.

questions.text: If only one X chromosome is found in a female person, which of the following symptoms will she show?
questions.subject: Science Reasoning

options.text: Small testis and absence of spermatogenesis
Presence of simian crease on the palm.

questions.explanation: If the female has only one sex chromosome, the female is suffering from Turner's syndrome. The genotype of the female having Turner's syndrome is 44A + XO. The symptom shown by the female having this disorder is the webbed neck. There is extra skin on the neck. There are other symptoms like short stature, infertility, swelling of hands and legs, etc. Thus, the correct answer is option B.

questions.text: During vertebrate development, the principle of induction is illustrated by:
questions.subject: Science Reasoning

options.text: The presence of a notochord beneath the ectoderm results in the formation of a neural tube
A neuron synapses with another neuron via a neurotransmitter
The neural tube develops into the brain, the spinal cord, and the rest of the nervous system
Secretion of TSH stimulates the secretion of the hormone thyroxine

questions.explanation: According to the principle of induction in vertebrate development, during the early development of tissues in a multicellular organ, one group of cells can lead to the establishment of developmental pathways for other responding tissues. Amongst the examples given, the statement that is in accordance with this principle is that the presence of a notochord beneath the ectoderm results in the formation of a neural tube. So, the correct answer is 'The presence of a notochord beneath the ectoderm results in the formation of a neural tube.'

questions.text: A human cell containing 22 autosomes and a Y chromosome is probably a:
questions.subject: Science Reasoning
questions.explanation: In humans, each cell nucleus contains 23 pairs of chromosomes, a total of 46 chromosomes. The first 22 pairs are called autosomes. The chromosomes of the 23rd pair are called sex chromosomes consisting of two X chromosomes in most females, and an X chromosome and a Y chromosome in most males. The X chromosome is always present as the 23rd chromosome in the ovum, while either an X or a Y chromosome can be present in an individual sperm. So the correct option is 'sperm cell'.

questions.text: Refer to the diagram below of a biochemical pathway. Arrows represent reactions catalyzed by the indicated enzyme. Letters represent chemical compounds consumed or produced during the reactions. If enzyme 3 can process compound C three times faster than enzyme 4 can process compound C, which of the following statements is TRUE?
questions.subject: Science Reasoning

options.text: Compound C will accumulate three times as much as compound E
Compound C will be consumed three times faster than compound B
Compound D will be consumed three times faster than compound E
Compound D will accumulate three times as much as compound E
Compound E will accumulate three times as much as compound D

questions.explanation: Compound C is being consumed in this reaction, so we can immediately eliminate choice (A). Furthermore, we have no information on the relative rates at which compounds B and C are being produced, so we can rule out choice (B). The biochemical pathway we have been given does not involve the degradation of either compound D or E, eliminating choice (C). If enzyme 3 operates 3 times faster than enzyme 4, we can expect that 3 times more product of enzyme 3 will be generated. The product of enzyme 3 is compound D, so (D) is the correct answer.

questions.text: The average number of students per classroom, y, at Central High School can be estimated using the equation y = 0.8636x + 27.227, where x represents the number of years since 2004 and x ≤ 10. Which of the following statements is the best interpretation of the number 0.8636 in the context of this problem?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning

options.text: The estimated average number of students per classroom in 2004
The estimated average number of students per classroom in 2014
The estimated yearly decrease in the average number of students per classroom
The estimated yearly increase in the average number of students per classroom

questions.explanation: When an equation is written in the form y = mx + b, the coefficient of the x-term (in this case 0.8636) is the slope of the graph of this equation in the xy-plane. The slope of the graph of this linear equation gives the amount that the average number of students per classroom (represented by y) changes per year (represented by x). The slope is positive, indicating an increase in the average number of students per classroom each year. Choice C is incorrect and may result from a misunderstanding of slope. You may have recognized that slope models the rate of change but thought that a slope of less than 1 indicates a decreasing function.

questions.text: For a lemonade stand, the total cost c, in dollars, of selling n cups of lemonade is given by c = 100 + 1.5n, What is the best interpretation of the number 100 in this equation?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning

options.text: The cost of each cup of lemonade
The number of cups of lemonade sold on the first day
The initial cost of setting up the lemonade stand
The maximum total cost of running the lemonade stand

questions.explanation: Because the slope is 1.5, 1.5 must be the cost associated with producing each cup of lemonade. The number 100, then, must be a cost not associated with each cup of lemonade. Instead, it is the initial cost of setting up the lemonade stand, when 0 cups of lemonade have been sold. Answer (D) is incorrect because the total cost increases as more lemonade gets sold; there is no maximum.

questions.text: During a trip, Jonathan had driven a total of 75 miles by 6:20 PM and a total of 85 miles by 6:40PM. He drove at the same rate for the entire trip. At what time had he driven a total of 140 miles?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning
questions.explanation: It took him 20 minutes to drive 85 -75 = 10 miles, which means he drove 1 mile every 2 minutes. At 6:40 PM, he needed to drive an additional 140 - 85 = 55 miles to get to the 140-mile point.

questions.text: A certain pizza restaurant cuts slices out at every 4 inches along the edge of a pizza. What is the maximum number of full pizza slices that can be cut out from a circular pizza with a radius of 10 inches?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning
questions.explanation: The circumference of the pizza is 2πr = 2π(10) = 20π, which is approximately equal to 62.83. We divide this value by 4 to get the maximum number of slices that can be cut out: 62.83 / 4 = 15.7 Since the slices must be full slices, the answer is 15.

questions.text: A sports equipment manufacturer produced 3,600 footballs and 2,200 basketballs during the fall. In the winter season, it produced 3,060 footballs and a certain number of basketballs. If the manufacturer decreased the production of basketballs by the same percentage as it did for footballs, how many basketballs did it produce in the winter?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning
questions.explanation: Calculating the percent decrease for footballs: (3,060 - 3,600)/3,600 which equals to =-0.15= 15% decrease. Calculating the number of basketballs produced, 2,200(1-0.15)=2,200(0.85)=1,870

questions.text: Richard has seven times as many pineapples as Fred. Nathan has three times as many pineapples as Fred. Richard has 32 more pineappleles than Nathan. How many pineapples does Fred have?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning
questions.explanation: If Fred has x pineapples, then Richard has 7x and Nathan has 3x. 7x = 3x + 32 4x = 32 x = 8

questions.text: To determine whether low lighting affects reading speed, a researcher randomly selected 30 subjects to participate in a study. Half of the subjects were randomly assigned to read an article under low lighting (400 lumens) while the other half read the same article under normal lighting (900 lumens). The resulting data showed that the subjects who read the article under low lighting took significantly longer than those who read the article under normal lighting. Based on the design and results of the study, which of the following is an appropriate conclusion?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning

options.text: Low lighting is harmful to the eyes
Everyone reads slower under low lighting
Low lighting is likely to cause a decrease in reading speed
High lighting (1,200 lumens) is likely to cause an increase in reading speed

questions.explanation: Based on the design of the study (random sampling and random assignment), we can conclude a cause and effect relationship: low lighting is likely to cause a decrease in reading speed. However, we can't say that this is true for every single person (like answer (B) does). Nor can we guess the effect of high lighting on reading speed when the study didn't involve high lighting (answer (D)). We also can't jump to the conclusion that low lighting harms the eyes (answer (A)).

questions.text: A train travelling at an average speed of 80 miles per hour takes 8 hours to complete a given trip. How much time would it take the train to complete the same trip if it traveled at an average speed of 120 miles per hour?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning
questions.explanation: The total distance traveled by the train on the given trip is 80 x 8 = 640. At a speed of 120 miles per hour, the same trip would take 640 / 120 = 5 ⅓ hours, or 5 hours and 20 minutes (a third of an hour is 20 minutes).

questions.text: A radioactive element loses 15 percent of its mass and 20 percent of its velocity. By what percent has its kinetic energy decreased?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning

questions.text: At a movie theater, an adult ticket costs $10 and a bag of popcorn costs $6. If a group of adults bought tickets to a movie and 4 bags of popcorn, what expression could be used to determine how much in total the group spent, in dollars?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning

options.text: 10x + 6, where x is the number of adults
10x + 24, where x is the number of adults
16x, where x is the number of adults
6x + 10, where x is the number of bags of popcorn purchased

questions.explanation: The adults spent 10x on tickets and 4( 6) = 24 dollars on popcorn. That's a total of 10x + 24, where x is the number of adults.

questions.text: On Friday, Janice read x pages every 30 minutes for 4 hours, and Kim read y pages every 15 minutes for 5 hours. Which of the following represents the total number of pages read by Janice and Kim on Friday?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning
questions.explanation: There are 8 30-minute segments in 4 hours so Janice read 8x pages. There are 20 15-minute segments in 5 hours so Kim read 20y pages. Altogether, they read 8x + 20y pages.

questions.text: Victor wants to sell 3 watches, each at the same price. After negotiating with customers, however, he ends up selling one at a 30% discount, one at a 20% discount, and one at a 10% discount. If Victor sold the watches for a total of $240, how much money did he lose by giving the discounts?
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning
questions.explanation: Let x be the original price of each watch. 0.7x + 0.8x + 0.9x = 240 2.4x = 240 x = 240/2.4 = 100The original price of each watch was $100. Victor would have received 100 x 3 = $300 had he not given the discounts. But because he did, he lost 300 - 240 = $60.

questions.text: The lengths of the three sides of a triangle are different prime numbers. If two of the sides have lengths 3 and 11, which of the following could be the length of the third side? I.7 II.13 III. 17
questions.subject: Mathematics Reasoning
questions.explanation: Seven can't be the third side because 7 + 3 < 11 (any two sides of a triangle must sum up to be greater than the third side). Seventeen can't be the third side because 3 + 11 < 17. The only valid length for the third side is 13 (II only).
